text:
# University Policies & Regulations Knowledge Base (Markdown)

> **Purpose:** Knowledge base for RAG chatbot answers about university rules, procedures, and regulations.

> **Note:** Synthetic content for project simulation; replace with official ERU handbook when available.
title: Section 0

text:
id: POL-0001
title: "Minimum Attendance Requirement"
category: "Attendance"
tags: ["attendance", "eligibility", "absence"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Minimum Attendance Requirement

text:
## Policy Statement

Students are expected to attend scheduled lectures, labs, and tutorials. Attendance is recorded per session and may be used to determine eligibility for assessments.

Attendance requirements aim to support learning continuity, fair evaluation, and timely academic feedback.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for attendance management.

**Rule:** Attendance below the minimum threshold may result in ineligibility to sit the final exam unless an approved exception exists.

## Common Questions

**Q:** What is the minimum attendance required?

**A:** A common minimum requirement is 75% attendance per course. Exact thresholds may vary by faculty and course syllabus.

**Q:** What if I miss a lecture due to illness?

**A:** Submit an excused-absence request with supporting documentation according to the faculty procedure, ideally within the stated time window.

**Q:** Does arriving late count as absent?

**A:** Late arrival may be recorded as late and can count against attendance depending on course rules.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 3

text:
id: POL-0007
title: "Quiz Policy"
category: "Assessment & Exams"
tags: ["quiz", "assessment"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Quiz Policy

text:
## Policy Statement

Assessments (quizzes, midterms, projects, finals) are conducted according to published course plans. Students must follow exam instructions and meet assessment deadlines.

Any accommodation or special arrangement must be requested through the official procedure before the assessment date.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for assessment & exams management.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Can I take a make-up exam?

**A:** Make-up exams are typically permitted only with approved justification (e.g., medical/emergency) and within official timelines.

**Q:** How do I request a regrade?

**A:** Submit a regrade request via the official form, including the question numbers and justification, within the regrade window.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.

text:
id: POL-0015
title: "Assignment Submission"
category: "Assignments & Submissions"
tags: ["assignment", "submission"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Assignment Submission

text:
## Policy Statement

Assignments must be submitted through the LMS before the due date/time stated by the instructor. Submissions are time-stamped and linked to the student account.

Students are responsible for verifying upload completion and preserving a local copy of the submitted work.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for assignments & submissions management.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Can I submit after the deadline?

**A:** Late submissions may be accepted with penalties or may be rejected depending on course policy.

**Q:** What if the LMS upload fails?

**A:** Take evidence (screenshot/time) and contact the course instructor or support desk immediately; do not wait until after the deadline.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.

text:
id: POL-0020
title: "Plagiarism"
category: "Academic Integrity"
tags: ["integrity", "plagiarism"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Plagiarism

text:
## Policy Statement

Academic integrity is a core university principle. Students must submit original work and appropriately acknowledge all sources and contributions.

Misconduct may lead to penalties that range from grade reduction to course failure or disciplinary action, depending on severity and recurrence.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for academic integrity management.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Is using AI tools allowed?

**A:** AI tool use depends on instructor policy; students must follow course instructions and cite tools when required.

**Q:** What happens if plagiarism is detected?

**A:** Cases are reviewed; penalties depend on severity and may include zero marks or disciplinary action.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 60

text:
## Policy Statement

Academic integrity is a core university principle. Students must submit original work and appropriately acknowledge all sources and contributions.

Misconduct may lead to penalties that range from grade reduction to course failure or disciplinary action, depending on severity and recurrence.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for academic integrity management.

**Guidance:** If AI tools are allowed, students should disclose usage (tool name, purpose) and ensure final work reflects their understanding.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Is using AI tools allowed?

**A:** Cases are reviewed; penalties depend on severity and may include zero marks or disciplinary action.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 75

text:
id: POL-0026
title: "Course Registration Window"
category: "Registration & Records"
tags: ["registration", "deadline"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Course Registration Window

text:
## Policy Statement

Course registration is managed through the academic portal during the announced registration window. Students must meet prerequisites and comply with credit-load limits.

Registration changes outside the official periods require approval according to university policy.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for registration & records management.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Can I add a course late?

**A:** Late registration is usually restricted and requires approval; use the add/drop period whenever possible.

**Q:** What if I do not meet prerequisites?

**A:** Enrollment may be blocked or removed unless an exception is approved by the department.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 78

text:
id: POL-0033
title: "Code of Conduct"
category: "Student Conduct & Services"
tags: ["conduct", "discipline"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Code of Conduct

text:
## Policy Statement

Students must maintain respectful behavior toward peers, staff, and instructors. Disruptive conduct may be subject to disciplinary review.

Student services policies ensure fairness, safety, and equal access to learning resources.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for student conduct & services management.

## Common Questions

**Q:** How can I report a problem?

**A:** Use the official grievance/complaint channel or contact student affairs. Include dates, details, and evidence where available.

**Q:** How is my data protected?

**A:** Student data should be handled according to privacy rules and accessed only by authorized roles.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 99

text:
id: POL-0039
title: "QR Attendance Verification"
category: "QR Attendance & LMS Use"
tags: ["qr", "attendance", "security"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: QR Attendance Verification

text:
## Policy Statement

The LMS provides secure access to academic services such as attendance, materials, announcements, and assessments. Users must protect their credentials and follow acceptable use rules.

QR attendance is used to reduce manual errors and provide transparent attendance records.

**Scope:** This policy applies to all students enrolled in courses where the LMS is used for qr attendance & lms use management.

**Verification:** The system may validate lecture time, student enrollment, and duplicate scans; suspicious patterns can be flagged for review.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Can someone scan the QR for me?

**A:** No. Proxy attendance is misconduct; QR attendance is tied to account identity and may be audited.

**Q:** What if my phone battery dies?

**A:** Inform the instructor/support desk; alternative verification may be available under course rules.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 117

text:
id: POL-0044
title: "Academic Probation Rules"
category: "Registration & Records"
tags: ["probation", "standing", "gpa"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Academic Probation Rules

text:
## Policy Statement

Students whose term GPA falls below the faculty threshold may be placed on academic probation. Probation status may restrict credit load and requires an improvement plan.

## Common Questions

**Q:** How do I exit probation?

**A:** Meet the required GPA threshold over the next term(s) as defined by your faculty and follow advisor recommendations.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 132

text:
id: POL-0045
title: "GPA Calculation"
category: "Registration & Records"
tags: ["gpa", "grading", "records"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: GPA Calculation

text:
## Policy Statement

GPA is calculated based on course credit hours and grade points. Repeated courses may follow the university repeat-policy (e.g., latest grade counts).

## Common Questions

**Q:** Do repeated courses replace the old grade?

**A:** Replacement rules differ by university; some use the latest grade, others average attempts.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 135

text:
id: POL-0046
title: "Appeal Process (General)"
category: "Student Conduct & Services"
tags: ["appeal", "procedure", "committee"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Appeal Process (General)

text:
## Policy Statement

Appeals must be submitted in writing through the official channel within the stated time window. Appeals should include evidence and clearly state the requested outcome.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Can I appeal any decision?

**A:** Appeals are accepted for eligible decisions defined by policy; not all academic judgments are appealable.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 138

text:
id: POL-0047
title: "Data Retention"
category: "Student Conduct & Services"
tags: ["data", "privacy", "retention"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Data Retention

text:
## Policy Statement

Academic records and LMS logs are retained for a defined period to support auditing, transcripts, and system improvement. Access is limited to authorized roles.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Who can access my records?

**A:** Only authorized staff (e.g., instructors for their courses, admins for system maintenance) and the student themselves.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 141

text:
id: POL-0048
title: "Notification Policy"
category: "QR Attendance & LMS Use"
tags: ["notifications", "announcements", "deadlines"]
source: "LearnUp Synthetic University Policies & Regulations Handbook"
last_updated: "2026-03-04"
version: "1.0"
title: Notification Policy

text:
## Policy Statement

The system sends notifications for deadlines, announcements, and attendance updates. Students are responsible for checking the LMS regularly, even if notifications are muted.

## Common Questions

**Q:** Can I disable notifications?

**A:** You may adjust preferences, but critical announcements may still be shown within the LMS.

## Notes

- This policy text is synthetic for graduation-project simulation; replace with your university's official handbook when available.
- If there is any conflict, the official university regulations take precedence.
title: Section 144
